reviews.review_description:
My wife's review. I'll add mine later.
---
I am very much enjoying PowerBeatsVR as my go-to alternative to a cardio VR fitness app I’d been using until January, when they announced they would no longer be updating it. I wasn’t a fan of how that company handled the changes, and it was also expensive since it required a subscription, and you never actually owned the app.

We found this app, and because it was so affordable, and we’d own it outright, it felt like a no-brainer to try. I’m very happy we did. I now have a great use for the panoramic photos we’ve taken over the years while hiking and walking in the woods. It’s been a lot of fun, and definitely worth adding the custom environments add-on. Even with those, it’s still incredibly affordable.

We’ve started incorporating it into our weekly fitness routine, and I’m excited to use it enough to unlock the hammers. It’s more engaging than I expected.

The other fitness app I used for years had a 360° spin-around-your-mat experience, which was enjoyable, but it meant two of us couldn’t really work out in the same room at the same time. There was a forward-facing option, but I rarely used it. Since this app is forward-facing by design, we can comfortably work out together in the same space, and I’ve found spinning in a circle isn’t really necessary.

Our panorama photos look amazing in-app. One environment we captured on a hiking adventure along the Gibraltar Trail in Nova Scotia feels like four environments in one, since the app lets you choose which direction you face while working out. Some days, I point myself toward the prominent rock feature since it’s a really immersive experience.

The workouts are very intense and fun. I especially liked the option to use your own music and generate workouts from your playlist—this is something I’ve missed in all the other workout games I’ve tried. It’s also great that there’s an audio cue when you hit an obstacle, so you immediately know you made a mistake, and that you can choose which hand to use for each target.

On the downside, the punching physics sometimes feel unnatural. For example, if you miss a target with one hand but it lightly touches your other hand, it still registers as a hit even without any real force.

reviews.review_title: Music sucks - work out is ok
reviews.review_description: I love Supernatural music (60's, 70, 80 and new). I love their stick work out. Power beats work out isn't bad. Don't like the hammers, nor ropes. Some songs have a LOT of squats but that is fine. But can not get in to it because the music is the worst! Can sing with it, I don't find it as upbeat. I am "older" and want singable music when I work out. Won't purchase anymore or this pack. Good side...it was available in Mexico where Supernatural isn't!

reviews.review_title: program is not easy to navigate
reviews.review_description: Spending more time figuring out navigation then working out, workouts so far are coupled of minutes long? Clearly I need to spend more time understanding navigation, but when I was new to the Supernatural app I needed no time in figuring it out and has been a much easier app to navigate with easy to understand long workouts

I've tried other VR exercise programs and PowerBeats VR is in the top 2 on my list. Why? Because it makes me sweat! The movements work every part of my body and I am having fun doing it!  I'm too busy busting targets and dodging concrete walls to even remember I'm working muscles I didn't even know I had! 😄  Now, I'm getting my 76-yr old dad into it!

Still loads of fun, and it will make you sweat. I downloaded a bunch of my own tunes (around 700 or so), and I can get them close to matching the swings. Probably i could get them to match, but close is good enough for me. I also have a few workout songs at 130 BPM (disco - don't laugh), and they match perfectly, but even the non-perfect matches are enjoyable. Try doing some of these workouts to the classics like Bizet or Shostakovich.I like the different modifications you can make, too (more squats, sidesteps, etc.). As far as the 'energy flow' and the hammer and shields and whatnot, nice to have but I don't use them. I'm not doing this for points, I'm doing it for the workout. So, if you want a good, energetic workout, that's a bunch of fun and doesn't come with a subscription,  this is it. It's a 5 star app for sure.

Tbh I forgot I bought this when I was looking for workout games that would hit the trifecta of full movement exercises, satisfying gameplay and the ability to use it with the music I love. This is near perfect. I don’t doubt once I tinker around with the options as I play more it will incorporate into my fitness routine nicely…AND No bloody subscription. I really like it!

developer_response.body:
Hi, glad to see you like it! 
Keep punching, keep sweating and having fun!

reviews.review_description: My wife and I workout everyday with Powbeats VR. Together! We each have a headset and our boundaries fit in one room. We punch, squat and sweat to the music. We've added 169 of our favorite songs to choose from. Love it! We're in our 60s BTW!

developer_response.body:
Glad to hear you like it! 
Keep punching, squatting and sweating, well keep it coming!

reviews.review_description: I can play my own mp3s, change bpm and create playlists. The only downside is that I can not step aside to avoid some walls as I have no space enough in my room. Should they make thinner walls, maybe :)

I really really enjoy this game, but I wish it showed my score/stats/stars somewhere for each song/level. I like to try and get perfect scores, but it doesn't show me that anywhere I can see unless I "set a new high score".

developer_response.body:
Hi, glad to hear you like it!

We will take your input into our backlog and see what we can improove here in the future.  Currently only the last highscore for each song is recorded permanently.  As a short term solution try using the screenshot function (button) on the endscreen of each song to save those results as a picture you can look up.  

Keep working out and having fun!

reviews.review_description: Admittedly I am not a fan of some of the graphical decisions (ie the tunnels), the other features outweigh this. Right off the bat you can import your own music, change the in-game features and it has an inbuilt editor. It's great. No subscription and add-ons are reasonably priced.

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
just a little hint:  In the Gameplay Menu in the Modifiers panel you can just select (checkmark) "No Tunnels" and they're gone 😜

Keep punching and having even more fun!

reviews.review_description: It won't work on my 3s I've uninstalled it and reinstalled it but nothing.get to the first screen says make boundary and also says tutorial but won't go pass that.
Great for cardio and also a great stress reliever. We have already gotten six people to run out and buy one.
I'm so glad I started using this program again.  I really enjoy our and easy to get back into.

Workouts are only 2 to 3 mins long. Become
 repetitive and boring

developer_response.body:
Hello, sounds like you're missing out on a lot of fun.
There are several ways to easily create and play new, longer or completely customized workouts.

- Add your own music. If you add a long song you get a longer workout. You can either have the generator create the workout for it with the standard settings by just clicking "Play", or if you have certain preferences you have many adjustment options for generating a workout to a certain song. You can also generate new anytime you like.
To add your own music just click on the little "i" symbol in the song selection menu and follow the instructions.

- Use playlists.  in the Playlist menu on the right you can add multiple songs together to playlists.  For each playlist you can select "Endless" or "Endurance" mode, which will add songs together to longer workouts, or even to endless workouts.  By selecting "Shuffle" you can have a continuous workout combining songs in the list at random.  Of course this all works for your own added music as well, so you can easitly put together whatever you like!  

Hope you will give it another try!

reviews.review_title: A great workout and fun too!
reviews.review_description:
Okay, so I took the leap and purchased this app. I was skeptical at first, but it gave me a really great workout! I am slowly unlocking  modifiers, and the workouts are changing a bit. It gets a little more challenging as you go up in levels.

My hubby also purchased the app, and we stand side by side (with lots of space between) and workout together. We can add our own music and jam while getting in shape.

Hitting, squating, and dodging walls makes this app more fun than other workout apps I've tried.  Two big thumbs up!

reviews.review_title: Does the job but lots of flaws
reviews.review_description: The graphics are attractive (A). Plenty of customization, but missing some critical options (B). The songs are very generic, but not awful (C-). The basic game only comes with a small collection of songs - less than DDR or Rock Band (C-). Some programmed ball and obstacle patterns are fun, some just do the job, and some feel unnatural. The worst part is when the balls fly straight at your face and you can't see what's coming behind them because the balls are opaque (C-). The best part of the game is the ability to import your own music and play random patterns timed vaguely to the beat. The beat auto-detect system is trash but the customizing controls let you set the correct beat speed and offset (expect to spend some time doing this for every song). After playing this for several weeks, I've had to remove 50% of my songs because I just can't get the beat to match and I don't like it when the ball hits aren't timed with the music beat. Setting custom songs to not use crouching (horizontal walls, tunnels, etc.) also removes bad balls (which are fun to hit with my shield) but still send balls at crouch level - if I leave crouching on but turn off horizontal walls and tunnels in the basic game settings, I have frequent spaces of nothing where the non-existent horizontal walls and tunnels would have been -  so I am forced to either crouch with obstacles, tolerate lots of boring empty space so I can have bad balls, or play with a less interesting set of obstacles. Why wasn't the attention to detail in other parts of the game applied here? In summary, custom music is doable but takes a lot of work to set up and isn't as customizable as it appears (C+). Overall, the game is playable, but I think you'll get frustrated and bored with it fairly quickly unless you either pay for more songs/patterns (are they worth it?) or upload your own music (and spend time customizing).

This game is designed to be a workout and it definitely doesn't disappoint. It's a fun way to get your heart rate up with jumps, punches, squats, and intense  movements that will get you sweating and keep you there. It's similar to beat saber, but at the same time not. Beat saber is more focused on hitting the beat, when this game is focused on the movement.Track your calories and your time to create a full workout routine. In the world of gamified workouts, no doubt this one has all my five stars.

You will not be sorry! 
If you like Beat Saber, you’ll love this.
Play your own mp3’s, no more waiting for your favorite music to pop up in the store. Play with clubs (like sabers), fists (boxing) and more. Customize not only difficulty and speed,but tweak how much you want to dodge, squat, punch… you decide how much you sweat!
You can even customise how loud the sound effects are and upload your own 360 environments!
Do not doubt any longer, BUY THIS GAME!!!

developer_response.body: Glad to hear you love it!  Keep having fun working out!

reviews.review_description:
I can't wait to play Beat Saber everyday. It's fun by myself or with several friends. 
It's great exercise and keeps you moving to the beat of the music.
It is ok app and exercise game, but it little hard to bend over

New to VR and at 58, I’m trying to lose a lot of weight this app is absolutely fantastic.  I do this now every day as it takes me into a world that’s away from stuffy classes and gyms. The graphics are fantastic. The only thing I would like is a bit of different music otherwise well worth the money spent

Tried a lot of different fitness apps and never liked any of them or they require a subscription plan. Here you get a one time payment and then you get your heart pumping while you're having a lot of fun. 

Right after my first match I was hooked and uninstalled all other apps. So I highly recommend this !

I really like the game but sometimes it takes several tries to open. I have checked for updates thinking that was an issue. But it wasn't. Sometimes it takes 3 tries before I can get the game to open. Is there something going on behind the scenes? I am not having issues with other games.
Perfect blend of music, agility, fitness and cool environments! Very highly recommended!
I love 2 things about it: you can upload your own music, and you can customize the task.

I’ve tried a number of VR workout apps, and this one is by far my favorite. I can hit targets with either hand, add my own music, and even use passthrough during exercises. I love seeing how many calories I’ve burned after each game, and the short 5-minute workouts make it easy to mix up the speed and variety. On top of that, the price is unbeatable.

I like the idea but at this moment it’s a waste of money. I have only been able play two songs, the rest just glitch and throw me to the two tracks on the next page! I cannot select any other songs to play.

Very well designed app. Graphics and sound quality are amazing! . I loved seeing the Santa and the Reindeer in the "Sky" environment when playing it for the first time here in December 2025....DEVELOPER - will this holiday touch remain in place? I'd love to keep it there year round! (or maybe a setting to toggle it on /off)

developer_response.body:
Hi, glad to hear you like Santa and Rudolph!  

Currently they only come around during Christmas season each year, to keep fit for the delivery of all those presents, though we might put up decorations and invite other characters for other seasonal events in the future.  

Keep working out and having fun!

reviews.review_description: First night and I think it's great.

Great workouts. Without the need for and expensive subscription. And the icing on the cake... You can load your own download music files. No need to buy music packs. The algorithm syncs your music to the routine nicely. The developers did a great job of giving you a lot of options to make the program to your liking. These are the guys that I like to support. I'll probably buy their add-ons even if I don't need them. And they definitely deserve a 5-star review. Highly recommended. Get this and avoid all the rest.

reviews.review_title: Curious if the music sync is better
reviews.review_description: I liked the games build in music. But when it came to my own stuff it was sub par. Would repeat several parts and it didn't even feel like it went with the song beat at all. There was also periods of silence added to my mp3 songs that ended up with like 2 to 3 minutes of me swinging at stuff to no music. Which really showed off the repetition in what I was doing. Just curious if you have improved that aspect yet or not seeing as I can now make my own mp3 songs that would be perfect for this thing.

developer_response.body:
Hi, just some hints to maybe improove your experience with your own songs.  
Our app will and can not change or add anything on your music file.  The generator will create a separate layout file containing all the obstacles matching your song.  You can adjust what sort of patterns and moves will be used for generation by adjusting the generator settings in the menu.   There is also a built in editor, so if you don't like particular sections in a certain song, just jump in and change it.  
The generator algorithm detects the beat by itself, and will also play it back to you , and you can check it and make adjustments if it should not match for some reason.  
Maybe give our FAQ a look for more details, to optimize your experience.   
If you need more help, don't hesitate to contact our support and we will do our best to assist you!

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been a personal trainer for 27 years. I have a new client with bad knees which prevents her from doing most cardio exercises. Today after our strength training session I had her try Powerbeats. 
She loved it. She got her heart rate up without impact to her knees. She had fun which is important when choosing cardio exercises. 
She went home and bought a Quest headset. 
I love that you get a great workout and don’t have to worry about failing like Beat Saber. 
I’ve been playing everyday and it’s a great workout. Now when I play Beat Saber my heart rate drops so I use Beat Saber as a cool down.

reviews.review_title: Hands down the best experience AND value
reviews.review_description: Highly customizable, quality imagery, great workout, no subscription fees.  There is nothing not to love here.  10/10 sparks joy.

Initially, I was excited to get this because you can upload your mp3 files; however, I discovered that the VR generator is not very good with this app. The actions do not match the song beat. I did find that X-Fitness and discovered that does a great job at generating actions/movements.

developer_response.body:
Depending on the song it can be a challenge to detect the correct beat.  The generator will play back the detected beat, and you can check if it matches, and also make adjustments that better suit you.  Maybe give the FAQ a look for more details.  
I hope that will help you have lots of fun during your workouts!

reviews.review_title: So Much Fun and Great Stress Relief
reviews.review_description: This may be a fitness game, but the fun is what’ll make you stick with it.   There’s something so satisfying about punching and smashing flying rocks that I’m able to play for much longer than I can endure much less physical exercise normally.   The haptics make the game immersive and satisfying.   The music is great as well.  Whether you’re looking to get fit or just take out your frustration in a healthy way, this is the game for you.
Verygood game for health

The game had my feet planted right into my couch and the low balls into my floor so I missed a lot. I tried exiting out of the game, speaking with the Ai that asked me more questions than provided answers and gave me big huge blurbs to read instead of providing me with simple directions on how to recentre the game. The options the Ai provided were not simple enough and the feet are still planted into my couch so I can’t play the game.

developer_response.body: Hi, position, centering and recentering are provided to the app by the respective platform of the headset.  On the Quest hold the Oculus Button on the right hand controller and it will recenter and rotate to your current position.  Floor level has to be adjusted outside our app via the menu.  Not much we can do from within our app here.  Hope that helps you to get to having fun and working out!

reviews.review_description: I barely write reviews for anything but this game def deserves one!! Had so much fun since I downloaded it two months ago. Too bad I was having issues downloading my own music so I bought the music pack. It's def more fun with more music and more background (yes I purchased both add-ons!). If you are in Advanced mode, it burns around 30 calories per song and sometimes I had to catch my breath in the middle. No complaints at all. Great workouts and great value for money!

The app itself is ok, I went for this one specifically because it said I can add my own music. This is true but when I try playing it it says could not find metadata and nothing plays. This is on a quest 3. My expectation is no track analysis is taking place on the quest 3.  I copied the files to the Music folder on the device, it's not obvious if this is the right place for them but it seems sensible. Selecting any of the other options on the screen give me the same no metadata error.

The mp3s are from various sources and all play normally as files.

I'm tech savvy so know it's not an issue with what I've done.
I’ve tried the other fitness apps. This one is the best. Just buy the app and ur good to go. I did my first workout yesterday and I’m feeling it today!!

I only use my own music, and I've made some adjustments (the "streams" thing looked redundant to me... yet, some may enjoy it) - and now it's sweaty, dynamic, one of my to-go cardio training apps. I also bought the custom environments pack, and they’re a nice addition.
The choice of "weapons" is a mix between fitness and gaming. Only the default one - fists - is useful for the former. All the rest reduce the amount of movement. A fitness addition would be something like *catching* some balls and throwing them (say, sorting them into two baskets, left and right?)
The maps are generally very nice. The maps are visually appealing and immersive, but there’s room for improvement in the pattern variety. The double balls always start in the middle, go to left lower corner, then right upper corner. Single balls are rare, and the sequence only occasionally feels truly random and surprising. Sometimes it's good - there are patterns that indicate a horizontal wall or a bad ball. Incidentally, 45 degrees walls would be a great addition. 
Another thing is that the portal is fixed in one place. It would add challenge and prevent zoning-out if it could move, randomly, around 30-40 degrees each side. 
The sequences mostly pair well with the music. But I hereby submit a petition to respect the fade-out in the music and NOT to throw in any energetic squatting sequence when the music is serenely fading out X) A warm-up at the beginning and a wind-down at the end could be a good idea. 
Overall, a great up - I totally recommend buying it. And hoping for further updates :)

The game stopped leveling up. I was at level eleven now I’m back at level one. And it went just over half way and now it’s not moving and it’s been a week and it’s still in the same spot. What can I do.  I really love the game at 65 it keeps me motivated please help

reviews.review_title: Thats realy nice and a challenging game
reviews.review_description:
I purchased this game for fun, and now I use it to warm up before training. My kids and I love this game! It has a lot of great features—for example, you can customize your training, add custom music, and explore additional worlds. Custom Environments addon is a must-have, it's offers plenty of new scenes to play in.
One thing I feel is missing: It would be great to have the option to create multiple profiles for kids so each could have their own statistics. That way, when I play a song, I could see both my best score and my kids' best scores, allowing us to compete.
Great fun and gets you fit in the process. Takes away having to go to a gym so you can get fit at your own pace with no on lookers. Brilliant absolutely recommend 100%

I’m 50 and no longer in the greatest shape after an ankle surgery from playing soccer and killing myself on obstacle courses between 35-45 but this game has me up and moving!! I absolutely love the challenge and the music. I’m thankful that I’m not continuously squatting but still able to incorporate them in my workout. 
The only modifier I do not like is using the  hammer instead of my fists. I wanted to try it for fun but I don’t think it tracked as well. Not a big deal since it’s just an option but figured I’d mention it. 

On a side note, I didn’t realize I can bring my own music into it so I’ll have to check that out. That said, I still very much enjoy the tracks offered plus the one I picked up.  
Highly recommend for a fun workout.

So this is one of the first games I purchased for my 3S because I wanted a good fitness game and this seemed to fit the bill. Played it for over a week and the workout was great. However, the songs wear on you very quickly. They are all these quasi-synthpop garbage songs you can hear at any tweener clothing outlet and overall are quite formulaic. In fact, I wouldn't be surprised if the developers created all of these in Suno using the exact same template(synthpop, autotune,hi-nrg,etc). I even purchased the extra song pack and yup, more of the same.

"But you can use your own music," you say? Yup. I know that and it was one of the selling points for me; and I thought that the game would have the ability to run it through some sort of AI algorithm and match up to at minimum the base BPM of the song. Nope, not even close. The objects are all over the place and not conforming to any sort of structure whatsoever. Very disappointing.

That being said, if you are into acts like Imagine Dragons,BTS,OneRepublic,Bastille, or any of that other formulaic pop garbage that use the basic "boots and cats" beat you will love this game. If you prefer even a smidgen of variety if your workout playlist, you had best look elsewhere.

As a millennial I have a trove of MP3s, and now they have been dusted off and transferred onto my Meta quest for use with this ruddy awesome fitness game. 

Personally I prefer it over BeatSabre, and love the way I can pick the exercise type ratios when setting up custom songs... Eg my old man knees cannot handle more than a 2 on the squat scale 🤣 but happy to punch as many rocks as you throw at me!

Some great built in music tracks for the price, and although doesn't have the downloadable packs of popular artists that other fitness games have, the ability to use your own music files is a game changer... Thanks Devs 👏👏

I am not easily motivated but this game had me wanting to keep going despite how tired I was.  My heart was thumping and I was sweating. Purchased it on sale and it was worth it. I’ve tried a few workout games similar to this one. This workout game is my favorite so far based on price and quality of workout. Some of the others have better graphics and music but I can upload my own music to this one and the graphics are good enough for me.

Story first—I am 57 and always looking for more to workout to.  I was at 310 pounds January of this year.  Boxing completely changed my life.  I lost 117pounds by boxing for 30minutes a day and eating nothing but homemade meals.  Nothing from a package.  Now about the app—What caught my eye with PowerBeats is the ability to put your own music to it.  This is cool!  Way too much fun!  I lost about three pounds not trying to and had to gain it back as I am at the point now of maintenance! Way to go developers!!

reviews.review_title: Nice… one suggestion… hope the developer
reviews.review_description:
Hi I purchased this a month or two ago.. it’s really good .. NO NON SENSE in this … pure workout
I want to suggest please remove the phrase like GODLIKE … people like me who are religious get offended by such words or make it customise to remove
And people like atheist might also get offended because they don’t want to be like someone whom they don’t believe… so it doesn’t serve them purpose… mad also it’s a MAJOR BLOCK for me to recommend this to my friends… thank you .. hope you see this.. please

I love how this workout is so customizable, I have seizures and can not do a whole lot with VR. You can download your own music, but instead I just open my prime music on my oculus. it will take the place of the music in the workout, but you still here the hits and the encouragements, I think that is pretty amazing. I was extremely sore after the first few days, and you sweat. I like that it is quick to access, so if I want a quick workout before work it's ok.  Also my husband does it, so I can wipe down and hand the headset right to him and from the workout menu, no extra buttons to hit, we can change my 4'11" to his 5'11", in a couple sec. No need to calibrate. I cancelled my Supernatural subscription, bc this is great and maybe a better workout. I suggest adding the 3.99 pack to it., so many worlds that are gorgeous. I use the bats more bc the boxing has to much light exploding in my face, where I Am light sensitive, but my husband loves the boxing. There is also a shield you can unlock. It's so neat, like if you do the world with the castle, the bats change to the environment, so cool. This is a one time purchase, and so great. I hope they come out with extra add ons, bc I will buy them also. Such a good price for a workout that does what it does. Also the movements are responsive, some of the other workouts on VR lag, Yes I would recommend.

reviews.review_title: Favorite of all the Fitness app
reviews.review_description: I really really super like the game. I just wish that they will develop the game and support hand tracking. For users that are hydrolysis like me, who everyday sweaty hands and feet 🐾 it's really great if it's controller less for fitness 🙂

I was sold on someone else’s review that said it’s better than Beat Saber. So, I’m glad I tried it out. This is very easy to get into, the music is great, and the scenaries are so good! On my very first workout, I did passthrough, chose an environment, picked a song, and selected advanced level. Ooh boy, my thighs are hurtin from all the squatting and weaving side to side! And hitting with the beats was absolutely fantastic. Everything was really great cardio with some muscle toning. Absolutely worth it. If there was just one workout app to sell skeptic non-VR ppl, this would have to be it.

This game was a total disapppointment. Lots of positive reviews but I struggled with this game for one reason specifically ... there is absolutely no weight to the balls whatsoever. After playing FitXR and Les Mills ... the targets feel like you are hitting something, they have weight ... this game feels 100% like punching balloons. It is totally underwhelming ... I am genuinely shocked this hasn't been raised as an issue ...Devs need to address this ASAP.

I play a lot of beat Saber.  I wouldn’t necessarily say this game is better than beat Saber as far as gaming but as a workout and being fun at the same time, it is definitely worth your time. You can definitely feel like you went to the gym after playing this game for 10 minutes, I would definitely recommend it
I like the app a lot, but would love to have a multiplayer version.

After the payment for the subscription the   Systems wasn’t activated … then I placed the refund request  the refund request was rejected. 

Still waiting do the refund..!

reviews.review_title: This is How You Game-ify Cardio Workouts
reviews.review_description: After just two sessions I understand how this is going to kick my cardio workout up a notch! After three songs in the second session, I was winded like I haven't been in a long time. It's almost like a tabbata workout, but much more fun! With the added capability to add your own songs, and edit the workout, it can also be heavily customized. As well, this is NO-IMPACT cardio! A bonus for those of us who have issues with our joints. Can't go wrong with this.
No subscriptions, no annoying coaches, just good exercise fun.
Bad graphics and non realistic. Going for fitxvr
Wow!!!! I just started this fitness.. It's so addictive.

reviews.review_title: A Fun Fitness App I Can Trust
reviews.review_description: FitXR. Let Me Down and got greedy. Les Mills. Limited Music Options. Beat Saber? Only releases select songs they choose for you unless you're in the modded community which comes under a lot of scrutiny.  PowerbeatsVR? My answer. My solution. My happy place.  The biggest and best thing is the one thing every workout app should let you do from jump: Use your own music. These devs answered the call. And I'm so happy I was here when they did. I was starting to give up on my workouts in VR. I was starting to give up on VR period. But the devs at Powerbeats gave me a reason to hold on a bit longer. They synchronize the atmosphere with your music too. And if you use the MQ3, you've got mixed reality mode too. 5 stars ladies and gentlemen. They get all 5 stars from me.

The best feature is that you can upload your own mp3s. This basically gives you unlimited songs to choose from. I keep coming back to this app (will play 2-3x/week) when I'm not feeling the gym on a given week or day. It's such a good workout, too. If you play for 15+ minutes on difficult/fast songs, you will feel it the next day!! Highly recommend for replay value and quality of workout. Must upload your own music to make it worth it!!

This game is the same "punch orb" base that's been around since Beat Saber.  The music is dull, and unengaging.  The overall "workout" experience is super basic, easily forgettable as it's the exact same platform 100s of other "fitness" apps are offering. Currently requesting a refund.  This 4.6 rating is a scam, majority of the reviews are either paid propaganda, or developer friends

reviews.review_title: Well worth the money! Great exercise!
reviews.review_description:
Edit: A year later and I still love this app! I have several exercise apps that I rotate through, but I ALWAYS finish my routine with this app. (I did discover you can remove the obstacles, like walls and "bad" balls, coming towards you, which helped me a lot when I first started.)

Original review: Wow, this is a great workout! I play several tennis games, but this is more exercise bang for your buck. I am REALLY feeling it in my legs and glutes. I did accidentally put it on expert once and just about killed myself, as I am only at the beginning level. I forgot I could hit a button and go back to the menu. :) Very much enjoying this app. Thanks so much to the developers!
I love power beats! It's an amazing work out, but the music selection isn't that good.

reviews.review_title: I mean why the good reviews? :/
reviews.review_description: I love using VR for workouts and I use it on a daily basis my FitXR and others too. I am also VERY competitive and push my limits to the roof. (I did boxing and other sports in my whole life) I was soooo excited to get my hands on this game but it was a total disappointment 😭 I hate leaving bad reviews but I’m hoping the developers are take these reviews as a learning opportunity.. My main reason I bought this is that you can use your own songs BUT the built in songs are just AWFUL 😣 literally I couldn’t find just 1 song that I like.. they aren’t made for exercise for sure. Other than the bad music I don’t like that I am unable to complete properly other than the beginner level, maaaybe at some songs the  advanced level cause it’s so hard to follow the instructions on higher levels if you have limited space for example and the obstacles are coming way too fast like in Beat Saber the only difference is that while there you are pretty much moving your hand only, here you have to react with your whole body. It’s really just makes it hard to enjoy your workout and do it properly in the same time. I will give this another try this weekend and try to upload my own music to see if it’s more enjoyable that way but if not then I’ll stick with FitXR & Lesmills and hoping for an update or I’ll wait for another fitness game which allows you to upload your own music.

I was hoping for something more like supernatural without a subscription.  The reviews were very misleading.  The included musics and workouts were terrible.  Not synced to the music and very few choices.  I had planned to download my own music and buy the extra environments, but this was too disappointing to continue with it.

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear you had problems.  Our songs are very precisely matched to the music, so my guess is there was another problem.  Feel free to contact us and we will gladly assist to solve issues.  
Give it another try!

reviews.review_title: Great WO, no subscription fee and…
reviews.review_description:
…the best part you can easily add your own music.   Love the developers for allowing this! 

Instructions: 

1. Plug your c cable into the computer 
2. Put headset on and plug other end into headset and click yes to the pop up asking to allow the connection. 
3. On your pc, click on the oculus in my computer (may look like a cell phone).  
4. Navigate to the music folder and drop ur favorite tunes. 
5. In PowerBeats click on the folder option in the main menu and simply scroll down to the music folder and select the tunes u want to smash balls too.

Editor’s choice!? What… I downloaded this game with high expectations. After playing Les Mills, supernatural, Fit XR… you would think the bar is high and this is on the same level, it’s not. It feels like this was designed 5 years ago. And you have to agree to not be able to get a refund when you buy. Pfff editor’s choice must be more like “paid for editor’s choice”. The physics and graphics are poor and do not get close to the other good sport games. It’s kind of like a copy of Super natural done with a small budget. 

I’m sorry to write a review that is negative. Probably there is a lot of work behind this,  it the bar is high right now so this should not cost anyhh to big above $5 if it can’t be refunded. 

Super low value. I guess it impresses new ve people who don’t know about the  good apps? So confused about the good reviews…. I’ll b claiming this purchase anyways, it feels like a scam.

I was very excited to get this game, but I can't seem to get the feel for it.
I've tried some other workout intended apps, some felt better than the others, but none of them managed to frustrate me as this one did.
At first, I thought, wow, I'm in terrible shape, which is true, but I think I'm not so bad because I can finish my workouts in all of the other apps I've tried, so why I can't keep up with the speed this game has?.
Some songs feel really well synced, some others just don't, especially at the higher levels of difficulty. The easy setting is too easy, while the other settings can get frustrating, you see a lot of balls and walls coming at you so fast that you can't keep up… or at least I can't.
Maybe it's not the game, maybe it's just that I'm in a terrible shape after all and this game is not for me at this moment.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear you had issues.  Maybe give the FAQs a look, there are tons of options to adjust nearly everything, and then give it another chance?

reviews.review_description: Didn't enjoy at all, deleted

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear you didn't like it.  
You are welcome to ask us and give us feedback anytime, so we know how we can improve the experience for you and everyone else.  
Feel free to contact us with your problems, and maybe give it another chance.

reviews.review_description:
Pros:
 No monthly subscription
 Smooth gameplay
 Personalized playlist

Con: 
 None as far as I am concern

I’m a 56 year old heart attack and quadruple bypass surgery survivor. I am relentless on my exercising daily. I’m always looking for new ways to do my cardio workout and after trying my son’s oculus I just bought my own and found this app. I get a great workout, I’ve just started so I have to unlock more features as I go. What I’m hoping for is that something with high knee lifts will be added. I’m also an ambassador for the American Heart Association of Washington so I’m hoping to bring workouts like this to others in the future.

This game has a nice selection of different styles of music and certainly got me up and moving. The three difficulty levels allow an easy start with headroom for a more challenging game at the higher level. A lot of fun and (as a not very fit bloke in his mid fifties) I felt like I'd done a reasonable amount of exercise after 30 minutes playing.

This game is awesome!
Good to exercise in my room.

This is a great app for fitness! Allows you to play to your own music which is fantastic. I played a lot on the first day and now I am sore, will be taking it easier now. But that means it really helps, so I am not complaining... too much... 😉

This game really works up a sweat. I love it and it's fun. I use the game almost every day. It doesn't get boring. I started playing this game because of the fact that it didn't use any subscriptions and in my opinion it looks much better then subscription based games.
Awesome game! Usually I hate cardio, but with this game I only realize I’m working out because I need to breathe hard 🤣
